- VEEVAVAULT:INSUFFICIENT_ACCESS
- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Delete Object Records

`<veevavault:delete-object-records>`

Deletes object records in bulk from the selected vault object using object metadata provided in CSV
or JSON format.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Metadata Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CSV</p></li><li><p>JSON</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Metadata format.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Vault object metadata in CSV or JSON format as an Input Stream to create bulk object records on the vault.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA
- VEEVAVAULT:RESPONSE_TIMEOUT
- VEEVAVAULT:IOEXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:PARAMETER_REQUIRED
- VEEVAVAULT:CONNECTIVITY
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_AUTH
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DOCUMENT
- VEEVAVAULT:USERNAME_OR_PASSWORD_INCORRECT
- VEEVAVAULT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- VEEVAVAULT:JSON_EXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:ATTRIBUTE_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED
- VEEVAVAULT:METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:NO_PERMISSION
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SPARK_MESSAGE
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_TOKEN
- VEEVAVAULT:MALFORMED_URL
- VEEVAVAULT:INSUFFICIENT_ACCESS
- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Export Documents

`<veevavault:export-documents>`

Queries a set of documents to export to your vaultâs FTP staging server.

Use the **Export Documents** operation in a separate asynchronous flow by using Async Scope and
passing the payload with document IDs. When used asynchronously, the **Export Documents** operation
executes in separate flow thread and waits for the specified polling interval to poll the job status
until it is successful while the main flow continues its process.

The FTP staging area in the folder stores the exported documents, with the
`{root}/{userId}/{jobId}/{documentIds}/{versions}` hierarchy structure.

The following example shows the **Export Documents** operation returning job results as a response:

\[ { "file": "/41601/249051/0_1/TestDocument.docx", "user_id\_\_v": 1885110, "id": 249051,
"responseStatus": "SUCCESS", "minor_version_number\_\_v": 1, "major_version_number\_\_v": 0 }, {
"file": "/41601/249050/0_1/TestDocument.docx", "user_id\_\_v": 1885110, "id": 249050,
"responseStatus": "SUCCESS", "minor_version_number\_\_v": 1, "major_version_number\_\_v": 0 }, {
"file": "/41601/249052/0_1/TestDocument.docx", "user_id\_\_v": 1885110, "id": 249052,
"responseStatus": "SUCCESS", "minor_version_number\_\_v": 1, "major_version_number\_\_v": 0 } \]

This example contains the following attributes:

- `file`  
  Uses the `"/{jobId}/{documentId}/{major-minor-version}/{filename}"` format, which is the absolute
  file path location on the Vault FTP server staging area.
- `user_id__v`  
  Vault system user ID of a user whose credentials are configured in the connector. The folder is
  created on the Vault FTP server staging area root location under where the exported file is
  located. The folder is created with the name of the user ID prefixed with the letter `u`, for
  example `/uXXXXXXX/{jobId}/{documentId}/{major-minor-version}/{filename}\)`.
- `id`  
  Document ID of the exported document file.
- `major_version_number__v`  
  Major version number of the exported document file.
- `minor_version_number__v`  
  Minor version number of the exported document file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Metadata Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CSV</p></li><li><p>JSON</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Metadata format.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Document Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Document metadata in CSV or JSON format as an Input Stream to export documents from vault.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>To exclude source files, set to <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Renditions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>To include renditions, set to <code>true</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>All Versions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>To include all versions or the latest version, set to <code>true</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Polling Interval</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Poll the vault at the specified interval, in seconds, until the job is successful.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA
- VEEVAVAULT:RESPONSE_TIMEOUT
- VEEVAVAULT:IOEXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:PARAMETER_REQUIRED
- VEEVAVAULT:CONNECTIVITY
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_AUTH
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DOCUMENT
- VEEVAVAULT:USERNAME_OR_PASSWORD_INCORRECT
- VEEVAVAULT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- VEEVAVAULT:JSON_EXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:ATTRIBUTE_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED
- VEEVAVAULT:METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:NO_PERMISSION

- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Get Audit Details

`<veevavault:get-audit-details>`

Retrieves all audit details for a specific audit type. Place a For-Each/Splitter element after this
operation to fetch each JSON data (page) in sequential order.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Audit Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Audit type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Start Date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Start date to retrieve audit information. This date cannot be more than 30 days ago. Dates must be of <code>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ</code> format. Dates and times are in UTC. If the time is not specified, it defaults to midnight (T00:00:00Z) on the specified date.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>End Date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>End date to retrieve audit information. This date cannot be more than 30 days ago. Dates must be of <code>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ</code> format. Dates and times are in UTC. If the time is not specified, it defaults to midnight (T00:00:00Z) on the specified date.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of limits or records per page.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Batch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of pages per batch.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of Binary payload and <a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:USERNAME_OR_PASSWORD_INCORRECT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:JSON_EXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:ATTRIBUTE_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED
- VEEVAVAULT:METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:NO_PERMISSION
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SPARK_MESSAGE
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_TOKEN
- VEEVAVAULT:MALFORMED_URL
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA
- VEEVAVAULT:RESPONSE_TIMEOUT
- VEEVAVAULT:IOEXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:PARAMETER_REQUIRED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_AUTH
- VEEVAVAULT:INSUFFICIENT_ACCESS
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DOCUMENT
- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Get Document Renditions Types

`<veevavault:get-document-renditions-types>`

Retrieves document rendition types using the document ID metadata in JSON format.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Document Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Document ID metadata in a JSON format payload, for example, <code>{"documentId": "534"}</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA

- VEEVAVAULT:CONNECTIVITY
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_AUTH
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DOCUMENT

- VEEVAVAULT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- VEEVAVAULT:JSON_EXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:ATTRIBUTE_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED
- VEEVAVAULT:METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:NO_PERMISSION
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SPARK_MESSAGE
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_TOKEN
- VEEVAVAULT:MALFORMED_URL
- VEEVAVAULT:INSUFFICIENT_ACCESS
- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Get Documents

`<veevavault:get-documents>`

Retrieves document details from Veeva Vault. You can either:

- Provide document properties (document type, subtype, and classification) to retrieve document
  details.

  If document properties are provided, the **Get Documents** operation builds the VQL query
  dynamically. The VQL query is then executed on the vault to retrieve document details.

- Have document properties (document type, subtype, and classification) fetched to retrieve document
  details.

  If document properties are not provided, the document properties metadata is fetched using
  `/api/{version}/metadata/objects/documents/properties` and the VQL query is built based on the
  document metadata properties added or inserted in the **Document Properties** list. If metadata
  properties are not provided, then all queryable document properties are used and executed in the
  VQL query.

Place a For-Each/Splitter element after this operation to fetch each documentâs data (page)
sequentially.

This operation provides a paging mechanism based on Mule standard pagination. Refer to
[Streaming and Pagination](veevavault-connector-config-topics#streaming_and_pagination.adoc) for
more details.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Document type to retrieve the type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SubType</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Document subtype to retrieve the type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Classification</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Document classification to retrieve the classification.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Document Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of document properties to build a VQL query.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>WHERE Clause</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>WHERE clause that is appended to dynamically build a VQL query.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Fetch size which limits the records per page.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Batch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Batch size which accommodates the number of pages in a specified number of batches.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of Binary payload and <a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:USERNAME_OR_PASSWORD_INCORRECT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:JSON_EXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:ATTRIBUTE_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED
- VEEVAVAULT:METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:NO_PERMISSION
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SPARK_MESSAGE
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_TOKEN
- VEEVAVAULT:MALFORMED_URL
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA
- VEEVAVAULT:RESPONSE_TIMEOUT
- VEEVAVAULT:IOEXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:PARAMETER_REQUIRED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_AUTH
- VEEVAVAULT:INSUFFICIENT_ACCESS
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DOCUMENT
- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Get Object Records

`<veevavault:get-object-records>`

Retrieves object records details using object metadata fields to build a VQL query dynamically and
execute on the vault to get object details. Place a For-Each/Splitter element after this operation
to fetch each object records (page) sequentially.

This operation provides a paging mechanism based on Mule standard pagination. Refer to
[Streaming and Pagination](veevavault-connector-config-topics#streaming_and_pagination.adoc) for
more details.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Fields</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object fields list to include in the dynamic VQL query.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>WHERE Clause</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>WHERE clause.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of limits or records per page.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Batch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of pages per batch.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of Binary payload and <a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:USERNAME_OR_PASSWORD_INCORRECT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:JSON_EXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:ATTRIBUTE_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED
- VEEVAVAULT:METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:NO_PERMISSION
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SPARK_MESSAGE
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_TOKEN
- VEEVAVAULT:MALFORMED_URL
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA
- VEEVAVAULT:RESPONSE_TIMEOUT
- VEEVAVAULT:IOEXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:PARAMETER_REQUIRED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_AUTH
- VEEVAVAULT:INSUFFICIENT_ACCESS
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DOCUMENT
- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Get Picklists

`<veevavault:get-picklists>`

Retrieves all available values configured on a picklist.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Picklist Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Picklist name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Invoke REST API

`<veevavault:invoke-rest-api>`

Invokes generic Veeva Vault REST APIs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Method</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GET</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li><li><p>POST</p></li><li><p>PUT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP method to send the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>GET</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path in which the request is sent, for example, <code>/vobjects/{object_name}/actions/{action_name}</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>/</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Body of the request message.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP headers the message must include.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>URI Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URI parameters that are provided in the <strong>Path</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Query parameters the request must include.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Streaming Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the request must be sent using streaming or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Query

`<veevavault:query>`

Executes the specified VQL query on the vault and retrieves the result in a paginated data input
stream. Place a For-Each/Splitter element after the Query operation to fetch each record in
sequential order.

This operation provides a paging mechanism based on Mule standard pagination. Refer to
[Streaming and Pagination](veevavault-connector-config-topics#streaming_and_pagination.adoc) for
more details.

> [!NOTE] Do not specify `LIMIT/PAGESIZE` and `OFFSET/PAGEOFFSET` in the VQL query. These parameters
> are incorporated internally.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>VQL Query</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>VQL query required as per Veeva API documentation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Input parameters in the format <code>#[{'name': 'Hemant', 'nickname': payload, 'company': vars.company}]</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of limits or records per page.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Batch Size (DEPRECATED)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of batches of <strong>Fetch Size</strong> elements to return. This field is deprecated.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of Binary payload and <a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:USERNAME_OR_PASSWORD_INCORRECT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:JSON_EXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:ATTRIBUTE_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED
- VEEVAVAULT:METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:NO_PERMISSION
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SPARK_MESSAGE
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_TOKEN
- VEEVAVAULT:MALFORMED_URL
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA
- VEEVAVAULT:RESPONSE_TIMEOUT
- VEEVAVAULT:IOEXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:PARAMETER_REQUIRED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_AUTH
- VEEVAVAULT:INSUFFICIENT_ACCESS
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DOCUMENT
- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Spark Validator

`<veevavault:spark-message-validator>`

Validates and verifies Spark messages triggered from the vault with a signature and public key. Upon
successful validation and verification, the Spark message can be further processed in the Mule flow
according to your business requirements. Refer to
https://developer.veevavault.com/sdk/#spark-messagingSpark Messaging feature and functionalities and
setup for more details.

The output of the **Spark Validator** operation includes Spark attributes (optional) with the Spark
message as it is received from the vault.

> [!NOTE] Before triggering a Spark message from the vault, make sure to raise a support ticket with
> Veeva Vault Support to increase the time interval (for example, 10 seconds) for the Spark message
> to resend or retry. By default, the time interval is configured as 5 seconds, which is less than
> the time required for the **Spark Validator** operation to complete message validation and
> verification.

> [!NOTE] As per Veeva Systems, the public key (00001.pem) expires every two years. You receive
> notification in advance advising you of the expiration date. If you are using the **Spark
> Validator** operation in a Mule application flow, after the public key expiration date you must
> remove the public key file from the path configured in the **Public Key Path** field. A new public
> key will be created during execution of the key removal. If you donât do this, Spark message
> verification fails as `INVALID_SPARK_MESSAGE`. The Mule application deployed on CloudHub with the
> default path must be restarted for successful Spark message verification.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Include Spark Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Flag to include Spark headers received from the vault in the Spark validator output.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Public Key Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Directory path to store the public key (00001.pem) file that is used to verify incoming Spark messages with a signature. Retrieved from the vault, if not specified.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${mule.home}/apps/${app.name}/publickeys</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Spark Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Spark headers received from the vault and embedded in the Spark message.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.headers]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Spark Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Input Stream</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Spark message body received from the vault.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA
- VEEVAVAULT:RESPONSE_TIMEOUT
- VEEVAVAULT:IOEXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:PARAMETER_REQUIRED
- VEEVAVAULT:CONNECTIVITY
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_AUTH
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DOCUMENT
- VEEVAVAULT:USERNAME_OR_PASSWORD_INCORRECT
- VEEVAVAULT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- VEEVAVAULT:JSON_EXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:ATTRIBUTE_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED
- VEEVAVAULT:METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:NO_PERMISSION
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SPARK_MESSAGE
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_TOKEN
- VEEVAVAULT:MALFORMED_URL
- VEEVAVAULT:INSUFFICIENT_ACCESS
- VEEVAVAULT:INACTIVE_USER
- VEEVAVAULT:SESSION_EXPIRED

### Unauthorize

`<veevavault:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that it is impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resource Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the resource owner for whom to invalidate access.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

### Update Documents

`<veevavault:update-documents>`

Updates bulk documents using editable metadata in the payload provided in CSV or JSON format.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Metadata Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CSV</p></li><li><p>JSON</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Metadata format.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Document Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Editable document metadata in CSV OR JSON format as an Input Stream to update single or multiple documents on the vault.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA
- VEEVAVAULT:RESPONSE_TIMEOUT
- VEEVAVAULT:IOEXCEPTION
- VEEVAVAULT:PARAMETER_REQUIRED

- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_AUTH
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DOCUMENT
- VEEVAVAULT:USERNAME_OR_PASSWORD_INCORRECT
- VEEVAVAULT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

- VEEVAVAULT:ATTRIBUTE_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VEEVAVAULT:OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED
- VEEVAVAULT:METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED

### Update Object Records

`<veevavault:update-object-records>`

Updates object records in bulk using object metadata in CSV or JSON format.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Metadata Format</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CSV</p></li><li><p>JSON</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Metadata format.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Vault object metadata in CSV or JSON format as an Input Stream to create bulk object records on the vault.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- VEEVAVAULT:API_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_KEY_FORMAT
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_SESSION_ID
- VEEVAVAULT:EXECUTION
- VEEVAVAULT:CLIENT_PROTOCOL
- VEEVAVAULT:INVALID_DATA
- VEEVAVAULT:RESPONSE_TIMEOUT
